titlestmt.titleproper: John Henry and Anna Botsford Comstock papers21-23-25
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-11-11 18:12:35 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: John Henry and Anna Botsford Comstock papers

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Creighton, Katherine. (Title of work: Nature songs and stories..)

did.physdesc: Correspondence, engravings, watercolors, photographs, slides, published materials, clippings, and notes.
did.abstract: Includes personal correspondence of Anna Botsford and Henry John professional correspondence; publications by or about the Comstocks; lecture and field notes; certificates and awards; typescript draft of an autobiography by Anna Comstock, published posthumously as The Comstocks of Cornell: John Henry Comstock and Anna Botsford Comstock; Botsford and Comstock family photographs; Anna Botsford diaries and poem; a scrapbook relating to the Botsford family, Phebe Irish Botsford, and Anna Botsford Comstock; materials concerning wood engraving; photographs and sketches (pen and ink and pencil).

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: John Henry Comstock was a professor of entomology at Cornell University. Anna Botsford Comstock was a professor of nature studies and a wood engraver specializing in scientific illustration.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes personal correspondence of Anna Botsford and Henry John (later John Henry) Comstock, 1859-1923; professional correspondence (1878-1926) documenting John Henry Comstock's influence in the development of entomology departments in colleges throughout the United States; publications by or about the Comstocks; lecture and field notes; certificates and awards; typescript draft of an autobiography by Anna Comstock, published posthumously as THE COMSTOCKS OF CORNELL: JOHN HENRY AND ANNA BOTSFORD COMSTOCK edited by Glenn W. Herrick and Ruby Green Smith, 1953; Botsford and Comstock family photographs; Anna Botsford diaries (1870-71, 1874-75) and poem; a scrapbook relating to the Botsford family, Phebe Irish Botsford, and Anna Botsford Comstock; materials concerning wood engraving; photographs and sketches (pen and ink and pencil) of insects by Anna Comstock used in Comstock Press publications; glass lantern slides used in Anna Comstock's nature study classes; wood block; engraving tools; and wood engravings of plants, insects, and nature scenes by Anna Botsford Comstock.
NATURE SONGS AND STORIES, by Katherine Creighton, illustrated by Anna Comstock, Comstock Publishing Company, 1914, autographed by Creighton and Comstock.
Woodcuts and drawings by others, presented to the Comstocks.

Bound manuscript "Jottings - Spiders," probably written by John Henry Comstock for Jennie Bartlett. Also, samples of various printing and engraving techniques, probably collected by Anna Botsford Comstock.
Photocopy of typescript letter from Henry Comstock to Charles Darwin regarding Comstock's report on cotton insects (June 4, 1880).

emph.ref: Series II. John Henry Comstock certificates and newspaper clippings
Series III. Anna Botsford Comstock materials
Series IV. Lecture and field notes
Series V. Anna Botsford Comstock autobiography
Series VII. Botsford and Comstock family photographs
Series VIII. Wood engravings, sketches, drawings, and photographs by Anna Botsford Comstock

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: John Henry and Anna Botsford Comstock papers, #21-23-25. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Anna Botsford and Henry John (later John Henry) Comstock personal correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Correspondence is physically arranged chronologically in the boxes but has been arranged alphabetically and then chronologically in this guide for easier search.

did.unittitle: John Henry Comstock certificates and newspaper clippings
Fellowship in American Association Advancement of Science
Membership in Societe Entomologique de France
California Academy of Sciences of San Francisco, honorary member
Elected member, American Association Advancement of Science
Elected fellow, American Association Advancement of Science
Elected fellow to American Entomological Society
Named member of permanent Committee of International Congress of Entomology
Honorary Fellow, Entomological Society of London
Honorary Fellow, Entomological Society of America
Correspondent, Academy of Natural Sciences, Philadelphia
Sociedade Entomologica de Brasil, Honorary membership
"Summer School of Entomology", Cornell Daily Sun
"Comstock Library Duly Presented as Memorial", Ithaca Journal
"Entomologists Save Many Crops from Bug Pests" Honolulu Star Bulletin
"Mrs Comstock Dies; Famous Many Years as Teacher, Artist", Ithaca Journal-News
"Comstock Rites 3pm Sunday Sage Chapel", Ithaca Hournal-News
"Professor J.H. Comstock, Entomologist, Dies" New York Times
Article on Professor Hiram Corson from the Troy Daily Times
The Theta Brook Song by Anna Botsford Comstock

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as The Comstocks of Cornell: John Henry and Anna Botsford Comstock, edited by Glenn W. Herrick Ruby Green Smith, 1953

did.unittitle: An Introduction to Entomology, John Henry Comstock
A Manual for the Study of Insects, John Henry and Anna Botsford Comstock
Ways of the Six Footed, Anna Botsford Comstock
Retirement of Professor John Henry Comstock, S.H. Gage
Reports om Scale Insects, John Henry Comstock
Trees at Leisure, Anna Botsford Comstock
The Common Butterflies: An Outline for Butterfly Study, Anna Botsford Comstock
"Professor John Henry Comstock" Annals of the Entomological Society of America
Resolution of the Trustees and Faculty of Cornell University on the Death of John Henry Comstock Emeritus Professor of Entomology
Resolution of the Trustees and Faculty of Cornell University on the Death of Anna Botsford Comstock, Professor of Nature Study Emeritus
Partners in Science, Alma Smith Payne

did.physdesc: Some photographs mounted on board, including cabinet cards and cartes-de-visite.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs include portraits of Anna Botsford Comstock and John Henry Comstock, their parents and relatives, as well as photographs of their home and gardens. Also included is a photograph of the first entomology building on Cornell campus (1888), and several group portraits of Cornell students and faculty.

did.unittitle: Oversized photograph of Anna Botsford's father

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Access only by permission of curator. Fragile, require careful handling.

did.unittitle: Anna Botsford Comstock in the garden

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From a Manual for the Study of Insects and Handbook of Nature Study by Anna Botsford Comstock

did.unittitle: Pen and ink sketches from the Handbook of Nature Study
Insect crawling up the letter P
Spider in a hot air balloon (letter I)
Photographs and sketches used in Comstock publications
did.physdesc: Most of these illustrations are mounted on board and include typewritten captions or handwritten notes about size for publishing.

did.unittitle: Photographs and sketches used in Comstock publications
did.physdesc: Many with labels or diagrammatic annotations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Used by Anna Botsford Comstock during her Nature Study classes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including a family of Flathead "Indians" and Cornell Insectary
